comments.author: @linkhant887
comments.text: Territorial Control: The military government controls a shrinking minority of the country, while rebel forces and ethnic armies have successfully seized vast expanses of territory, pushing the junta onto the defensive and leading to the imposition of forced conscription.Military Repression: Human rights monitors report systematic abuses by the military, including indiscriminate airstrikes, the burning of civilian homes, and brutal crackdowns on political prisoners.Failed Elections: The junta's implementation of military-controlled elections has been widely condemned by the United Nations as a polarizing tactic that worsens the violence rather than offering a credible path to peace.Humanitarian Catastrophe: Nearly a quarter of the population faces severe food insecurity, over 5 million people have been displaced, and the economy has lost roughly $100 billion since the coup began.

comments.author: @linkhant887
comments.text: Myanmar has been engulfed in a brutal civil war since a February 2021 military coup ousted the democratically elected government. The military junta is locked in a protracted conflict against a coalition of pro-democracy forces and ethnic armed organizations, which has resulted in mass displacement, a severe economic collapse, and widespread human rights violations

comments.author: @aml596
comments.text:
Where is the chapter about the devastation caused by the military junta across the country? Over 3.5,million people displaced, ~90000 killed, 20 million people in need of humanitarian assistance, hundreds of schools, churches, hospitals destroyed or damaged, ( bombed, shelled, burned down), tens of thousands houses destroyed, over 30000 political prisoners, forced conscription, massive digital surveillance?
Where is the chapter on the trafficking, scam centers, unregulated resource extraction, drug production, environmental damage and other illicit operations?

comments.author: @rhodiamann9057
comments.text: I was there in 1982. The people still wore traditional dress. There were no cheap Chinese imports. Local crafts abounded. I still have a set of beautiful lacquered trays which I use daily. Then, it was Burma. Now it is Myanmar. Guess which I prefer??

replies.author: @sherrielee8871
replies.text: Same as Cambodia now being Kampuchea. Burma and Cambodia are western, colonial names

replies.author: @VeteranExpat
replies.text:
I first came to Myanmar in 2014.  It was still beautiful at that time and 95% wore longyi and paso.  Now 70% wear ugly foreign clothing in Yangon.  The problem is YouTube is destroying them just like everywhere else.

Myanmar is a better name.
Burma was a colony.  They didn't choose to call themselves that.  The majority of people never lived in a country called Burma.

We just need our governments to stop punishing the Myanmar people for what the rich do.  We aren't hurting the corruptors.  We are hurting the poor.

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: It's fascinating to hear about your experience in 1982 and how things have changed. Thanks for sharing your perspective!

replies.author: @uulovelove
replies.text: I completely agree. Myanmar boasts stunning natural beauty, particularly its pristine seas accessible by boat from Thailand, alongside a rich, captivating culture. Sadly, the excessive ethnic fragmentation has fueled ongoing conflicts for decades, making a resolution highly unlikely in the near future. It is truly a pity, as a peaceful Myanmar would undoubtedly stand out as one of the top travel destinations in Southeast Asia
